mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical correlation with FEDFUNDS changes over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden spike in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points in a single month signals acute labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off selling and negative S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE spikes > 0.5pp, or the signal fails to predict direction with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal yields a negative or zero Rank IC over any 2-year rolling window, or earnings revisions do not follow the demand expansion pattern.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in the index of industrial production during a late-cycle expansion phase signals supply-side bottlenecks and profit margin compression, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions in late-cycle regimes produce positive 20-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no predictive power outside early-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly inflation reading below 2.5% confirms price stability, allowing equity markets to discount future cash flows at lower rates and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX returns are negative in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL MoM stays below 0.2%, or the signal loses predictive accuracy during high-volatility regimes.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between falling total nonfarm payrolls and rising unemployment indicates structural labor market weakness rather than cyclical adjustment, forecasting negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: The divergence signal yields positive 60-day SPX returns in > 55% of occurrences, or Rank IC remains negative over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly expands corporate revenue bases, mechanically supporting higher equity valuations and positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero correlation with 20-day SPX direction over a 5-year rolling window, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.

mechanism_narrative: Expansion in industrial production during a post-shock recovery regime signals successful capacity utilization and margin expansion, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansions in post-shock regimes produce negative 5-day SPX returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows no directional edge outside recovery phases.
